name: civics-cdf-validator

metadata.name: civics-cdf-validator
metadata.summary: Checks if an election feed follows best practices

documentation: civics_cdf_validator is a script that checks if an election data feed follows best practices and outputs errors, warnings and info messages for common issues.

modules.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator
documentation:
NIST CDF validation library.

A set of rules that can be used to validate a NIST 1500-100 file containing
election candidate or sitting officeholder data according to the included
XSD and additional higher-level requirements.

See https://developers.google.com/elections-data/reference/
Copyright 2020 Google LLC
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator._validate_path
documentation: Check that the files provided exist.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator._validate_rules
documentation: Check that the listed rules exist.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator._validate_severity
documentation: Check that the severity level provided is correct.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator._validate_country_codes
documentation:
Check that the supplied 2 country code is correct.

Check if the provided country code is listed in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes
pylint: disable=g-doc-args

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator.display_rules_details

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator.filter_all_rules_using_user_arg

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator.exec_profiling
documentation: This is a decorator to add profiling to the feed validation.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator.feed_validation
documentation: Validate the input feed depending on the user parameters.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.validator.print_exceptions_from_validations
documentation: Print errors to stdout.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.GpUnitOcdIdValidator

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.GpUnitOcdIdValidator.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the class.

If a list is provided, that list will be used to initialize the set of OCD
IDs. If a file is provided, the OCD IDs will be pulled from the local file.
Otherwise, the OCD IDs will be fetched from Github.

Args:
  country_code: the code for the country to fetch OCD IDs for
  local_file: the file containing the OCD IDs
  check_github: boolean that indicates if OCD IDs should be fetched from
    Github
  ocd_id_list: a list of OCD IDs

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.GpUnitOcdIdValidator.is_valid_ocd_id
documentation:
Check whether the given OCD ID is valid.

An OCD ID is valid if:
- it is in the list of existing OCD IDs
- it is properly formatted
- it has a valid country code

Args:
  ocd_id: the OCD ID of interest

Returns:
  True if the OCD ID is valid. False otherwise.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.GpUnitOcdIdValidator.is_valid_country_code
documentation: Check whether country code in the given OCD ID is valid.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.GpUnitOcdIdValidator.is_country_or_region_ocd_id
documentation: Check whether the given OCD ID represents a country or region.

Validates GpUnit OCD-IDs.

This class fetches and stores existing OCD IDs to be used for validity checks.
It should be initialized with the user input.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.OcdIdsExtractor

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.OcdIdsExtractor._read_csv
documentation: Reads in OCD IDs from CSV file.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.OcdIdsExtractor._get_ocd_data
documentation:
Returns a list of OCD-ID codes.

This list is populated using either a local file or a downloaded file
from GitHub.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.OcdIdsExtractor._get_latest_commit_date
documentation: Returns the latest commit date to country-*.csv.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.OcdIdsExtractor._download_data
documentation: Makes a request to Github to download the file.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.OcdIdsExtractor._verify_data
documentation: Validates a file's SHA.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.gpunit_rules.OcdIdsExtractor._get_latest_file_blob_sha
documentation: Returns the GitHub blob SHA of country-*.csv.

Extract OCD IDs from github or from a local file if defined.

The complete OCD ID definition can be found at
https://open-civic-data.readthedocs.io/en/latest/proposals/0002.html

modules.name: civics_cdf_validator.version
documentation:
Single source package version.

No dependencies should be added to this module.

See https://packaging.python.org/guides/single-sourcing-package-version/

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.get_parent_hierarchy_object_id_str
documentation: Get the elt path from the 1st parent with an objectId / ElectionReport.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.LogEntry
documentation: This class contains needed information for user output.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.ElectionException
documentation: Base class for all the exceptions in this script.
pylint: disable=g-bad-exception-name

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.ElectionFatal
documentation: An Fatal that prevents the feed from being processed successfully.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.ElectionError
documentation: An error that prevents the feed from being processed successfully.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.ElectionWarning
documentation:
An issue that should be fixed.

It will not stop the feed from being successfully processed but may lead to
undefined errors.
pylint: disable=g-bad-exception-name

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.ElectionInfo
documentation: Information that user needs to know about following XML best practices.
pylint: disable=g-bad-exception-name

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.ExceptionListWrapper

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.ExceptionListWrapper.exception_handler
documentation: Gather log entry counts by type, class, and total.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.loggers.ExceptionListWrapper.print_exceptions
documentation: Print exceptions in decreasing order of severity.

This provide helpers to manage a list of ElectionException.

This class contains a list of ElectionExceptions. It provides a handler to
enrich the list and manage the validator output depending on the user choice
for severity. So, as an example if the user selects a severity warning,
ElectionWarning, ElectionError log_entry will be displayed and ElectionInfo
will be skipped. We print the selected severity + other Exception types with
higher severity.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.SchemaHandler

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.SchemaHandler.get_element_class
documentation: Return the class of the element.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.SchemaHandler.strip_schema_ns
documentation: Remove namespace from lxml element tag.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.SchemaHandler.get_elements_by_class
documentation: Searches for all tags under element of type element_name.
Base class for anything that parses an XML schema document.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.BaseRule

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.BaseRule.elements
documentation: Return a list of all the elements this rule checks.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.BaseRule.check
documentation: Given an element, check whether it implements best practices.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.BaseRule.set_option
documentation:
Used to set commandline options for the rule.

Args:
  option: commandline option object.

Raises:
  ElectionException: the rule must have the option_name attribute.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.BaseRule.setup
documentation: Perform any rule specific setup before checking.
Base class for rules.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.TreeRule

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.TreeRule.check
documentation: Checks entire tree.
Rule that checks entire tree.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.ValidReferenceRule

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.ValidReferenceRule._gather_reference_values
documentation:
Collect a set of all values that are referencing a pre-defined value.

Ex: A party leader ID should reference an ID from a PersonCollection.
This method should return a set of all party leader IDs.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.ValidReferenceRule._gather_defined_values
documentation:
Collect a set of the pre-defined values that are being referenced.

Ex: A party leader ID should reference an ID from a PersonCollection.
This method should return a set of all PersonIDs from the PersonCollection.
Rule that makes sure reference values are properly defined.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.DateRule

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.DateRule.reset_instance_vars
documentation:
Reset instance variables to initial state.

Due to ordered procedure of validator, instance vars created in init
are not getting reset when same rule is run on different elements.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.DateRule.gather_dates
documentation:
Gather StartDate and EndDate values for the provided element.

An election element should have a start and end date in the desired format.
These dates should be extracted and set as instance variables to be used
in validation checks.

Args:
  element: A parent element that contains StartDate and EndDate children.

Raises:
  ElectionError: dates need to be properly formatted.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.DateRule.is_date_in_past
documentation: Check if a date is in the past.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.DateRule.check_for_date_not_in_past
documentation: Check if given date is not in past and add an error message to the error log if the date is in past.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.DateRule.check_for_date_in_past
documentation: Check if given date is in past and add an error message to the error log if the date is not in past.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.DateRule.check_end_after_start
documentation: Checks if EndDate is after StartDate and add an error message to the error log if the EndDate is before StartDate.

Base rule used for date validations.

When validating dates, this rule can be used to gather start and
end date values.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.PartialDate

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.PartialDate.init_partial_date
documentation: Initializing partial date.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.PartialDate.is_older_than
documentation:
Compares 2 dates/partial dates.

Args:
  other_date: date to be compared.

Returns:
  The difference between the years if the given dates only contains a year.
  The difference between the years if the given dates contains year and
  month, then when the years of both dates aren't same.
  The difference between the months, if the years of both dates are same.
  The difference between the days if the given dates contain complete day,
  and if the year and month of both dates are the same.
Check for PartialDate.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.MissingFieldRule

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.MissingFieldRule.get_severity
documentation: Return 0 for Info, 1 for Warning, or 2 for Error.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.MissingFieldRule.element_field_mapping
documentation: Return a map of element tag to list of required fields.
Check for required fields for given entity types and field names.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.RulesRegistry

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.RulesRegistry.register_rules
documentation:
Register all the rules to be checked.

Returns:
  A dictionary of elements and rules that check each element

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.RulesRegistry.count_stats
documentation: Aggregates the counts for each top level entity.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.base.RulesRegistry.check_rules
documentation: Checks all rules.
Registry of rules and the elements they check.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.get_external_id_values
documentation: Helper to gather all Values of external ids for a given type.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.get_additional_type_values
documentation: Helper to gather all nested additional type values for a given type.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.extract_person_fullname
documentation: Extracts the person's fullname or builds it if needed.

functions.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.get_language_to_text_map
documentation: Return a map of languages to text in an InternationalizedText element.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.Schema
documentation: Checks if election file validates against the provided schema.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.OptionalAndEmpty

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.OptionalAndEmpty.check
documentation: pylint: disable=g-explicit-length-test
Checks for optional and empty fields.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.Encoding
documentation: Checks that the file provided uses UTF-8 encoding.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.HungarianStyleNotation
documentation:
Check that element identifiers use Hungarian style notation.

Hungarian style notation is used to maintain uniqueness and provide context
for the identifiers.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.LanguageCode
documentation: Check that Text elements have a valid language code.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PercentSum
documentation: Check that Contest elements have percents summing to 0 or 100.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.EmptyText
documentation: Check that Text elements are not strictly whitespace.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DuplicateID
documentation: Check that the file does not contain duplicate object IDs.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidIDREF

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidIDREF._gather_object_ids_by_type
documentation: Create a mapping of element types to set of objectIds of same type.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidIDREF._gather_reference_mapping
documentation: Create a mapping of each IDREF(S) element to their reference type.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidIDREF._determine_reference_type
documentation: Determines the XML type being referenced by an IDREF(S) element.

Check that IDREFs are valid.

Every field of type IDREF should actually reference a value that exists in a
field of type ID. Additionaly the referenced value should be an objectId
of the proper reference type for the given field.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidStableID
documentation: Ensure stable-ids are in the correct format.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectoralDistrictOcdId
documentation: GpUnit referred to by ElectoralDistrictId MUST have a valid OCD-ID.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.GpUnitOcdId
documentation: Any GpUnit that is a geographic district SHOULD have a valid OCD-ID.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DuplicatedGpUnitOcdId
documentation: 2 GPUnits should not have same OCD-ID.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DuplicateGpUnits
documentation: Detect GpUnits which are effectively duplicates of each other.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.GpUnitsHaveSingleRoot
documentation: Ensure that GpUnits form a single-rooted tree.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.GpUnitsCyclesRefsValidation
documentation: Ensure that GpUnits form a valid tree and no cycles are present.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.OtherType
documentation:
Elements with an "other" enum should set OtherType.

Elements that have enumerations which include a value named other should
-- when that enumeration value is other -- set the corresponding field
OtherType within the containing element.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PartisanPrimary
documentation:
Partisan elections should link to the correct political party.

For an Election element of Election type primary, partisan-primary-open,
or partisan-primary-closed, the Contests in that ContestCollection should
have a PrimartyPartyIds that is present and non-empty.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PartisanPrimaryHeuristic
documentation: Attempts to identify partisan primaries not marked up as such.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.CoalitionParties
documentation: Coalitions should always define the Party IDs.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UniqueLabel
documentation: Labels should be unique within a file.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.CandidatesReferencedInRelatedContests
documentation:
Candidate should not be referred to by multiple unrelated contests.

A Candidate object should only be referenced from one contest, unless the
contests are related (connected by SubsequentContestId). If a Person is
running in multiple unrelated Contests, then that Person is a Candidate
several times over, but a Candida(te|cy) can't span unrelated contests.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ProperBallotSelection
documentation:
BallotSelections should be correct for that type of contest.

Ensure that the BallotSelection elements in a CandidateContest are
CandidateSelections, PartyContests have PartySelections, etc, etc.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.CorrectCandidateSelectionCount
documentation:
CandidateSelections should only reference one candidate.

This rule will throw a warning if a CandidateSelection references multiple
candidates or does not reference any candidates at all. We currently do not
support tickets (i.e. CandidateSelections with multiple candidates) except for
party list elections.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.SingularPartySelection
documentation:
Each PartySelection should have exactly one Party in the PartyIds.

While technically the schema allows multiple IDs, currently our pipeline does
not support this. Having multiple parties can cause undefined behavior.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PartiesHaveValidColors
documentation:
Each Party should have a valid hex integer less than 16^6, without a leading '#'.

A Party object that has no Color or an invalid Color should be picked up
within this class and returned to the user as a warning.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PersonHasUniqueFullName
documentation:
A Person should be defined one time in <PersonCollection>.

The main purpose of this check is to spot redundant person definition.
If two people have the same full name and date of birhthday, a warning will
be raised. So, we can check if the feed is coherent.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.BadCharactersInPersonFullName
documentation: A person Fullname should not include bad characters.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidatePartyCollection

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidatePartyCollection.check_specific
documentation: Return a list of info log to be raised.

Generic party collection validation rule.

All partyCollection validation rules can inherit from this class since it
contains basic checks.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidateDuplicateColors
documentation:
Parties under the same contest should have unique color.

A Party object that has duplicate color and referenced under the same contest
should be picked up within this class and returned to the user as a warning
message.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DuplicatedPartyAbbreviation
documentation:
Party abbreviation should be used once in a given language.

If an abbreviation is duplicated, the corresponding party should be picked up
within this class and returned to the user as an Info message.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.EmptyPartyAbbreviation
documentation: Party abbreviations should not be empty.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DuplicatedPartyName
documentation:
Party name should be used once in a given language.

If a party name is duplicated, the corresponding party should be picked up
within this class and returned to the user as an Info message.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MissingPartyNameTranslation
documentation:
All Parties should have their name translated to the same languages.

If there is a party name that is not translated to all the feed languages,
the party should be picked up within this class and returned to the user as
an Info message.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MissingPartyAbbreviationTranslation
documentation:
Every party's abbreviation should be translated to the same languages.

If a party is missing a name translation, it should be picked up within this
class and returned to the user as an Info message.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.IndependentPartyName
documentation:
Warns on parties that contain common names indicating they are an independent party.

These should instead supply the IsIndependent attribute.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DuplicateContestNames
documentation:
Check that an election contains unique ContestNames.

Add Warning if duplicate ContestName found.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UniqueStableID
documentation:
Check that every stableID is unique.

Add an error message if stable id is not unique

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MissingStableIds
documentation:
Check that each NIST object in the feed have a stable Id.

Add an error message if stable id is missing from the object.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PersonsMissingPartyData
documentation:
Each Officeholder Person should have a Party associated with it.

A Person object must contain a PartyId, and if not, it should be picked
up within this class and returned to the user as a warning.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.AllCaps
documentation:
Name elements should not be in all uppercase.

If the name elements in Candidates, Contests and Person elements are in
uppercase, the list of objectIds of those elements will be returned to
the user as a warning.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.AllLanguages
documentation:
Verify that required languages are present in Text fields.

The Text elements in all entities with those fields should cover all
required languages for this schema.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidEnumerations
documentation:
Valid enumerations should not be encoded as 'OtherType'.

Elements that have valid enumerations should not be included
as 'OtherType'. Instead, the corresponding <Type> field
should include the actual valid enumeration value.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MultipleCandidatesPointToTheSamePersonInTheSameContest
documentation: Raise an error when multiple candidates point to the same person in the same contest.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.SelfDeclaredCandidateMethod
documentation:
A self declared candidate cannot have an "electoral-commission" id.

Please update the candidate Pre election Status.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidateOcdidLowerCase
documentation:
Validate that the ocd-ids are all lower case.

Throw a warning if the ocd-ids are not all in lowercase.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ContestHasMultipleOffices
documentation: Ensure that each contest has exactly one Office.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PersonHasOffice
documentation: Ensure that each non-party-leader Person object linked to one Office.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PartyLeadershipMustExist
documentation: Each party leader or party chair should refer to a person in the feed.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ProhibitElectionData
documentation: Ensure that election data is not provided for officeholder feeds.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PersonsHaveValidGender
documentation: Ensure that all Person objects have a valid gender identification.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.VoteCountTypesCoherency
documentation: Ensure VoteCount types describe the appropriate votable.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.URIValidator
documentation:
Basic URL validations.

Ensure each URL has valid protocol, domain, and query.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UniqueURIPerAnnotationCategory

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UniqueURIPerAnnotationCategory._extract_uris_by_category
documentation:
For given list of Uri elements return nested paths of Uri values.

Args:
  uri_elements: List of Uri elements
Returns:
  Top level dict contains Annotation values as keys with uri/paths mapping
  as value.

Check that annotated URIs for each category are unique.

Ex: No ballotpedia URIs can be the same.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidYoutubeURL
documentation:
Validate Youtube URL.

Ensure the provided URL is not a generic youtube url or direct link to a
playlist as one of the invalid youtube URL types.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidTiktokURL
documentation:
Validate Tiktok URL.

Ensure the provided URL is a valid Tiktok URL types.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidURIAnnotation
documentation:
Validate annotations on candidate/officeholder URLs.

Ensure they describe the type of URL presented.
Throws Warnings and Errors depending on type of invalidity.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.OfficesHaveJurisdictionID
documentation: Each office must have a jurisdiction-id.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidJurisdictionID
documentation: Each jurisdiction id should refer to a valid GpUnit.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.OfficesHaveValidOfficeLevel
documentation: Each office must have a valid office-level.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.OfficesHaveValidOfficeRole
documentation: Each office must have a valid office-role.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ContestHasValidContestStage
documentation: Each Contest must have a valid contest-stage.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DateOfBirthIsInPast
documentation: Date of Birth should not be in the future.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectionContainsStartAndEndDates
documentation: Election elements should have start and end dates populated.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectionStartDates
documentation:
Election elements should contain valid start dates.

Start dates in the past should raise a warning. This is not an error
as validator could conceivably be run during an ongoing election.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectionEndDatesInThePast
documentation:
Election elements would be in the present or in the future.

Using a past end date needs to be notified. The warning is useful to prevent
using a wrong date.

A bounded election with a past end date is an error since the date should be
confirmed. The only exception to this is elections that are postponed or
canceled.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectionEndDatesOccurAfterStartDates
documentation:
Election elements should contain a coherent start and end dates.

End dates should not occur before the start date.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidPartyLeadershipDates
documentation:
Party Leadership start/end dates should be valid if specified.

End dates should not occur before the start date.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectionDatesSpanContestDates
documentation:
Election start/end dates should span the Contest start/end dates.

The start date of the Election should be on or before the start date of every
included Contest. The end date of the Election should be on or after the end
date of every included Contest. Only Contests that have the dates populated
will be considered.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectionTypesAreCompatible
documentation: Election element Type values cannot be both a general and primary type.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectionTypesAndCandidateContestTypesAreCompatible
documentation:
Election elements should contain CandidateContests with compatible types.

Elections with the general type should not have any CandidateContests with the
primary types. Elections with the primary types should not have any
CandidateContests with the general type.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DateStatusMatches
documentation:
In most cases, ContestDateStatus should match ElectionDateStatus.

If all contests contained in an election have the same status, and this status
does not match the status on the election, it is probably incorrect - raise
warning.  Differing values among ContestDateStatus in an election are possible
but uncommon - raise Info level message.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.OfficeTermDates
documentation:
Office elements should contain valid term dates.

Offices with OfficeHolderPersonIds should have a Term declared. Given
term should have a start date. If term also has an end date then end date
should come after start date.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.RemovePersonAndOfficeHolderId60DaysAfterEndDate
documentation:
Office elements should contain valid term dates.

Check if 60 days after the specified office term EndDate,
the associated Person and OfficeholderId may be removed from the feed.
However, if the Person is elected to another office,
they will be kept in the feed with the same stable ID.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UniqueStartDatesForOfficeRoleAndJurisdiction
documentation:
Office StartDates should be unique within a certain group.

Office StartDates should be unique amongst a group of Office entries
with the same jurisdiction-id and office-role.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.GpUnitsHaveInternationalizedName
documentation: GpUnits must have at least one non-empty InternationlizedName element.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ValidateInfoUriAnnotation
documentation:
InfoUri is an annotated Uri that accepts the following annotations.

wikipedia, ballotpedia, official-website, fulltext.
Adding a check for this.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.FullTextMaxLength

class_variables.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.FullTextMaxLength.MAX_LENGTH
documentation: about 8-10 pages of text, 4500-5000 words
FullText field should not be longer than MAX_LENGTH.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.FullTextOrBallotText

class_variables.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.FullTextOrBallotText.SUGGESTION_CUTOFF_LENGTH
documentation: about 3 paragraphs, 250-300 words
Warn if BallotText is missing and FullText is short.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.BallotTitle
documentation: BallotTitle must exist and should usually be shorter than BallotText.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ImproperCandidateContest

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ImproperCandidateContest._gather_contest_candidates
documentation: Return candidate ids for given contest element.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ImproperCandidateContest._gather_invalid_candidates
documentation: Return candidate ids that appear to be BallotMeasureSelections.
Detect CandidateContest elements that should be a BallotMeasureContest.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MissingFieldsError
documentation:
Check for missing fields for given entity types and field names.

Raise error for missing fields. To add a field, include the entity
and field in element_field_mapping.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MissingFieldsWarning
documentation:
Check for missing fields for given entity types and field names.

Raise warning for missing fields. To add a field, include the entity
and field in element_field_mapping.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MissingFieldsInfo
documentation:
Check for missing fields for given entity types and field names.

Raise info for missing fields. To add a field, include the entity
and field in element_field_mapping.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.PartySpanMultipleCountries
documentation:
Check if a party operate on multiple countries.

Parties can have PartyScopeGpUnitIds which span multiple countries. This is
sometimes correct, but we should flag it to double check.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.NonExecutiveOfficeShouldHaveGovernmentBody
documentation: Ensure non-executive Office elements have a government body defined.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ExecutiveOfficeShouldNotHaveGovernmentBody
documentation: Ensure executive Office elements do not have a government body defined.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MissingOfficeSelectionMethod
documentation:
Checks that SelectionMethod is present and .

Values supported for SelectionMethod:
-appointed
-directly-elected
-hereditary
-indirectly-elected
-succession
-ex-officio

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.SubsequentContestIdIsValidRelatedContest
documentation:
Check that SubsequentContests are valid.

Conditions for a valid SubsequentContest:
- OfficeId, if present, must match the original contest.
- PrimaryPartyIds, if present, must match the original contest.
- EndDate, if present, must not be after EndDate of the original contest.
- ComposingContestIds must not contain the original contest.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ComposingContestIdsAreValidRelatedContests
documentation:
Check that ComposingContestIds are valid.

Conditions for a valid ComposingContest:
- OfficeId, if present, must match the parent contest.
- PrimaryPartyIds, if present, must match the parent contest.
- ComposingContests must not be referenced by more than one parent contest.
- ComposingContests must not reference each other.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.MultipleInternationalizedTextWithSameLanguageCode
documentation: Checks for multiple InternationalizedText with the same language code.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.AllInternationalizedTextHaveEnVersion
documentation: Checks for Internationalized Text Elements missing the english version.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ContestContainsValidStartDate
documentation:
Contest elements should contain valid start dates.

A warning will be raised for start dates in the past. These dates are still
valid because the validator could be run during an ongoing election.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ContestContainsValidEndDate
documentation:
Contest elements should contain valid end dates.

A warning will be raised for end dates in the past. These dates are still
valid because the validator could be run during an ongoing election.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ContestEndDateOccursAfterStartDate
documentation: Contest elements should have end dates that occur after the start dates.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ContestEndDateOccursBeforeSubsequentContestStartDate
documentation:
Contest end dates should occur before subsequent contest start dates.

This rule will trigger if a Contest has a valid Subsequent Contest and both
Contests have dates.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ContestStartDateContainsCorrespondingEndDate
documentation:
Contest start dates must always have corresponding end dates.

A Contest can either have both StartDate and EndDate populated or neither at
all.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.CandidateContestTypesAreCompatible
documentation: CandidateContest Type values cannot have both a general and primary type.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.CommitteeClassificationEndDateOccursAfterStartDate
documentation: CommitteeClassification elements should have end dates that occur after the start dates.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.AffiliationEndDateOccursAfterStartDate
documentation: Affiliation elements should have end dates that occur after the start dates.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.EinMatchesFormat
documentation: EIN id should be in the following format: XX-XXXXXXX.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.AffiliationHasEitherPartyOrPerson
documentation: Affiliation should contain either a Party or a Person objectId.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.FeedTypeHasValidFeedLongevity
documentation: Feeds types should have valid corresponding FeedLongevity.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.FeedIdsAreUnique
documentation: FeedId should be unique.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.SourceDirPathsAreUnique
documentation: All SourceDirPaths should be unique.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.ElectionEventDatesAreSequential
documentation: Dates in an ElectionEvent element should be sequential.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.NoSourceDirPathBeforeInitialDeliveryDate
documentation: SourceDirPath should only be defined if one of the initial delivery dates is in the past.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.OfficeHolderSubFeedDatesAreSequential
documentation: Dates in an OfficeHolderSubFeed element should be sequential.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.FeedHasValidCountryCode
documentation: Feeds should have valid country code.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.FeedInactiveDateSetForNonEvergreenFeed
documentation: All non-evergreen feeds should have a FeedInactiveDate set.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UnreferencedEntitiesBase

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UnreferencedEntitiesBase._get_idref_elements
documentation: Returns the names of all XML elements in the schema of type IDREF or IDREFS.

methods.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UnreferencedEntitiesBase._gather_referenced_entities
documentation: Create a set of all entities referenced by either IDREF(S) elements or external identifiers.

All non-top-level entities in a feed should be referenced by at least one other entity.

In the context of this rule, top-level means that an entity is not expected to
be referenced by anything else in the feed. This base class allows derived
rules to specify the set of top-level and warning-level entities since these
differ by feed type. The rule is an info for gpunits (as long as they have
ComposingGpunitIds) since top-level gpunits may exist solely to contain
others.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UnreferencedEntitiesElectionDates
documentation:
CDF elections and contests are top-level in election dates feeds.

All other entity types must be referenced.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.UnreferencedEntitiesOfficeholders
documentation:
CDF offices and party leadership entities are top-level in officeholders feeds.

This rule is a warning for CDF parties since we ask for all parties for some
LatAm feeds.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.DeprecatedPartyLeadershipSchema
documentation:
Warns if the deprecated party leadership schema is used.

This rule will eventually become an error once feeds are migrated to the new
schema.

classes.name: civics_cdf_validator.rules.RuleSet
documentation: Names for sets of rules used to validate a particular feed type.
